messages.content: Finally! The kitchen is basically done. Ramirez finished the last cabinet door this morning and I'm sitting here with actual coffee from my actual coffee maker in my actual kitchen for the first time since October.
That must feel amazing after all these months! How did everything turn out compared to what you were expecting?

Honestly? Better than I hoped. The countertops look incredible - worth every day of that three week delay. Kevin surprised me too. Remember how worried I was about him doing the backsplash himself? Turns out he's got hidden tile skills. It looks professional.
That's wonderful! It sounds like Kevin really stepped up. What's the final damage budget-wise?

Oof. Forty-two five. So we went over the original 38K quote by about four and a half thousand. That surprise plumbing issue in February really got us. But honestly, at this point I'm just grateful it's 90% complete and we can actually cook again.
That's a significant overrun, but it sounds like you're handling it well. Will the extra costs impact your other financial plans much?

We'll absorb it. My bonus covered most of the base cost, and we had some buffer built in. The eating out budget the last five months probably added another grand on top of everything, but that's behind us now. I'm just ready to meal prep again and get back to normal routines.
It'll be nice to have your home base back. What's left in that final 10% to wrap things up?

Just touch-up paint and the final electrical inspection. Ramirez says we should have that done by Friday. Then it's officially official. I'm planning to cook a proper family dinner this weekend - first time since October that we'll all sit around our own table eating food from our own kitchen.

summary: Diane celebrates her kitchen renovation being 90% complete after months of work. The final cost came to $42,500, over the original $38,000 quote, but she's satisfied with the results including Kevin's successful DIY backsplash work.
